insert into `core_news_translations` (`locale`, `origin_id`, `title`, `content`, `create_user`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values ('en', 186, 'A firsthand review of a campsite in the forest in Cat Ba.', '

The forest camping areas in Cat Ba, especially in the core zone of the National Park and the Ao Ech area, offer a truly pristine experience with a diverse ecosystem and fresh air. To have a complete trip, you need to prepare basic trekking skills, equip a waterproof tent, and comply with nature conservation regulations. This real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba will guide you through a detailed itinerary and small tips to fully enjoy the beauty of Jade Island.

Why is the trend of forest camping in Cat Ba on the rise?

In recent years, instead of choosing luxury resorts, many people turn to real reviews of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba to plan a trip completely cut off from the outside world. Cat Ba is not only about the sea; it also boasts the largest special-use forest in northern Vietnam with unique karst limestone terrain.

Sleeping in the middle of the forest helps you regenerate your energy, breathe oxygen-rich air, and practice survival skills. This is the most effective "healing" therapy for souls worn out by work pressure.

Real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba: Which spots are the most ideal?

When it comes to pitching a tent in the heart of the great forest, we cannot fail to mention the names that have built the reputation of ecotourism here. Below are the most objective evaluations:

What makes the Cat Ba National Park area special?

This is the most official and safest spot for first-time campers. The National Park has areas specifically zoned for campers, ensuring no negative impact on the environment. Here, you can combine a trek up Ngu Lam Peak to admire a panoramic view of the island from above before setting up camp.

Ao Ech area: A pristine paradise in the heart of the island

If you want a more challenging real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba, Ao Ech (Frog Pond) is a destination not to be missed. This is the largest freshwater lake in the mountains of Cat Ba. The feeling of waking up by the lakeside, with mist drifting across the water and the constant croaking of frogs, is an extremely priceless experience.

Wild clearings near the forest edge for beginners

For groups with children or people who do not have much experience, choosing areas near the forest edge or camping sites managed by local residents is a reasonable choice. You still get a feeling of closeness to nature while ensuring there are basic support services.

Suggested 2-day, 1-night itinerary for your real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba

To make this real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba truly useful, let me share the itinerary my group followed:

Day 1:

  • 08:00: Travel from the center of Cat Ba town to the National Park gate.
  • 09:00: Buy tickets and start the trek through the forest.
  • 12:00: Lunch break at the Ao Ech area, with a light meal of food you brought along.
  • 15:00: Choose a flat spot, start setting up the tent and gathering dry firewood (only collect fallen dry branches).
  • 18:00: Cook by the campfire, chat, and stargaze.

Day 2:

  • 06:00: Wake up to greet the sunrise in the forest and brew some clean coffee.
  • 08:00: Clean up all litter thoroughly (the Leave No Trace principle).
  • 10:00: Trek back toward Viet Hai village or return to the National Park gate.

In addition, discover many high-quality travel experiences at Vietnamtoure.com

How much does forest camping in Cat Ba actually cost?

Based on the experience from real reviews of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba, the cost is usually very cheap but the value of the experience is extremely high:

  • National Park entrance ticket: About 80,000 VND/person.
  • Hiring a porter or guide (if needed): About 500,000 - 800,000 VND/group.
  • Food costs: About 200,000 - 300,000 VND/person (depending on the BBQ menu you prepare).
  • Equipment rental (if you don't have your own): A tent, sleeping bag, and flashlight cost about 200,000 VND/set.

In total, for just under 1 million VND, you can have a quality real review trip to the forest camping areas in Cat Ba.

Indispensable items when camping in the forest

Don't let your trip turn into a nightmare just because of poor preparation. In this real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba, I emphasize the following items:

  • Double-layer tent: The Cat Ba forest has high humidity and many mosquitoes, so a double-layer tent provides better waterproofing and insect protection.
  • Sleeping bag and insulating mat: Even in summer, nights in the forest are very cold.
  • Insect repellent: This is an absolute essential. Leeches and forest mosquitoes in Cat Ba are quite numerous, especially after rain.
  • Super-bright flashlight: There is no electricity in the forest, so make sure you have at least 2 light sources.
  • Water filter or bottled water: Water sources in the forest are not always safe to drink directly.

 

Real experience: How to ensure safety?

According to the content of this real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba, safety is the top priority. You should note:

  • Monitor the weather: Absolutely do not enter the forest when a storm or heavy rain is forecast, due to the risk of limestone landslides.
  • Travel in a group: At a minimum, go in a group of 3 or more so you can support each other if an incident occurs.
  • Notify the forest management: Let the forest rangers know the location where you plan to set up camp.
  • Fire-building skills: Only build a fire in safe places, far from low tree canopies, and put it out completely before going to sleep or leaving.

What is the fastest way to get to the camping area?

To carry out your real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba, you can travel in the following ways:

  • From Hanoi: Take a high-quality coach (Hanoi - Cat Ba) across the Tan Vu - Lach Huyen sea-crossing bridge, then take a ferry or cable car over to the island.
  • From Cat Ba town: Rent a motorbike or take a taxi to the National Park gate (about 15km away). A motorbike is the most flexible means of transport for exploring the forest-edge routes.

 

The meaning of "eco-healing" when camping in Cat Ba

We don't just go to see; we go to feel. Every real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba is a story about connection. When you turn off your phone and move away from wifi signals, you begin to hear your inner voice more clearly. That is exactly the core value that LIEN MINH VIET Virenaretreat always aims for – bringing people back to Mother Nature to heal the wounds of the soul.

Protecting the environment during the camping process is also part of the healing process. When you cherish nature, nature will give you absolute peace in return.

How to prepare food when camping in the Cat Ba forest

Eating in the middle of the forest needs to be compact yet still nutritious. Below are the preparation steps:

  • Prep at home: Beef and chicken should be marinated in advance and frozen in a small ice box.
  • Prioritize dry food: Hardtack, instant noodles, and sausages are wonderful lifesavers.
  • Vegetables: Choose types that are hard to crush, such as cucumber, carrot, and potato.
  • Fruit: Oranges and tangerines provide vitamins and help quench thirst well.

Frequently asked questions about camping in Cat Ba

Are there leeches when camping in the Cat Ba forest?

Yes, especially during the rainy season from May to August. You should apply leech-repellent on your shoes and socks before going.

Do you need a permit to camp?

If you camp in the area managed by the National Park, you need to buy a ticket and comply with the designated locations. Spontaneous camping deep in the core zone is not permitted, in order to protect biodiversity.

Which season is best for a real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba?

Autumn (September - November) is the most ideal time, with dry, cool air and few insects.

Is there phone signal in the forest?

The deeper you go into the valley, the weaker the phone signal becomes, or it disappears entirely. Be prepared to be completely "offline."

The journey to explore and write a real review of the forest camping areas in Cat Ba is not just an ordinary trip, but a valuable slow-living experience. We hope the detailed sharing above will help you feel more confident in your upcoming plan to conquer the great forest of Cat Ba. Don't forget to keep the environment clean so that this pristine beauty lasts forever for future generations.

If you are looking for a reliable companion to organize healing tours, professional and emotional camping trips, contact us right away:

LIEN MINH VIET International Trading Company Limited

Virenaretreat - Where Your Soul Is Healed !

Address: 102 My Dinh, Cau Giay, Hanoi

Phone: 0986662282

Email: support@virenaretreat.com Website: virenaretreat.com

Below are the preparation steps:</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Prep at home: Beef and chicken should be marinated in advance and frozen in a small ice box.</span></li>\r\n

    </ul>\r\n
    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 6pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Frequently asked questions about camping in Cat Ba</span></h2>\r\n

You should apply leech-repellent on your shoes and socks before going.</span></p>\r\n
    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 12pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Do you need a permit to camp?</span></p>\r\n
